From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American singer (born 1979)

This article is about the lead singer of Maroon 5. For other people named Adam Levine, see [Adam Levine (disambiguation)](/wiki/Adam_Levine_(disambiguation) "Adam Levine (disambiguation)").

Not to be confused with [Adam DeVine](/wiki/Adam_DeVine "Adam DeVine").

**Adam Noah Levine** ([/ləˈviːn/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*lə-VEEN*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"); born March 18, 1979) is an American singer, musician and television personality who is the lead singer, rhythm guitarist and sole continuous member of the [pop rock](/wiki/Pop_rock "Pop rock") band [Maroon 5](/wiki/Maroon_5 "Maroon 5").[[4]](#cite_note-stuart-4)

Levine began his musical career in 1994 with the band Kara's Flowers, for which he served as lead vocalist and lead guitarist. After the commercial failure of their only album, *[The Fourth World](/wiki/The_Fourth_World_(album) "The Fourth World (album)")*, the group was reformed in 2001 as Maroon 5 – with [James Valentine](/wiki/James_Valentine_(musician) "James Valentine (musician)") replacing him as lead guitarist. In 2002, they released their first album, *[Songs About Jane](/wiki/Songs_About_Jane "Songs About Jane")*, which went multi-platinum in the US; since then, they have released seven more albums: *[It Won't Be Soon Before Long](/wiki/It_Won%27t_Be_Soon_Before_Long "It Won't Be Soon Before Long")* (2007), *[Hands All Over](/wiki/Hands_All_Over_(album) "Hands All Over (album)")* (2010), *[Overexposed](/wiki/Overexposed_(album) "Overexposed (album)")* (2012), *[V](/wiki/V_(Maroon_5_album) "V (Maroon 5 album)")* (pronounced: "five"; 2014), *[Red Pill Blues](/wiki/Red_Pill_Blues "Red Pill Blues")* (2017), *[Jordi](/wiki/Jordi_(album) "Jordi (album)")* (2021), and *[Love Is Like](/wiki/Love_Is_Like "Love Is Like")* (2025). As part of Maroon 5, Levine has received [multiple accolades](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Maroon_5 "List of awards and nominations received by Maroon 5"), including three [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"). As a solo artist, he achieved concurrent commercial success with his guest appearance on [Gym Class Heroes](/wiki/Gym_Class_Heroes "Gym Class Heroes")' 2011 single "[Stereo Hearts](/wiki/Stereo_Hearts "Stereo Hearts")", which peaked within the top five of the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100").

From 2011 to 2019, and again in 2025 and 2026, Levine has been featured for eighteen seasons as a coach on [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC")'s reality talent show *[The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_TV_series) "The Voice (American TV series)")*. The winners of multiple seasons ([1](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_season_1) "The Voice (American season 1)"), [5](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_season_5) "The Voice (American season 5)"), and [9](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_season_9) "The Voice (American season 9)")) belonged to his team. In 2012, Levine made his acting debut as the recurring character Leo Morrison in the second season of the television series *[American Horror Story](/wiki/American_Horror_Story "American Horror Story")*. He also appeared in the films *[Begin Again](/wiki/Begin_Again_(film) "Begin Again (film)")* (2013), *[Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping](/wiki/Popstar:_Never_Stop_Never_Stopping "Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping")* (2016), *[Fun Mom Dinner](/wiki/Fun_Mom_Dinner "Fun Mom Dinner")* and *[The Clapper](/wiki/The_Clapper_(film) "The Clapper (film)")* (both 2017). Levine launched his eponymous fragrance line in 2013. The same year, he collaborated with [Kmart](/wiki/Kmart_(United_States) "Kmart (United States)") and ShopYourWay.com to develop his menswear collection. He also owns a record label, [222 Records](/wiki/222_Records "222 Records"), and a production company, 222 Productions, which produced television shows *[Sugar](/wiki/Sugar_(2018_TV_series) "Sugar (2018 TV series)")* and *[Songland](/wiki/Songland "Songland")*. In 2013, *[The Hollywood Reporter](/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")* reported that "sources familiar with his many business dealings" estimated Levine would earn more than $35 million that year.[[5]](#cite_note-lacey-5)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Adam Noah Levine was born on March 18, 1979, in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles")[[6]](#cite_note-6) to Fredric Levine, the founder of retail chain M. Fredric, and Patsy (née Noah) Levine, an admissions counselor.[[5]](#cite_note-lacey-5)[[7]](#cite_note-7) They divorced when he was seven and Levine underwent therapy.[[8]](#cite_note-huff-8) Growing up, he spent weekdays with his mother and weekends with his father.[[9]](#cite_note-JC-9) He has a younger brother.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Levine's father and maternal grandfather were [Jewish](/wiki/Jewish "Jewish"), while his maternal grandmother was a [Protestant](/wiki/Protestant "Protestant").[[11]](#cite_note-11) Levine is Jewish;[[12]](#cite_note-12) however, according to *[The Jewish Chronicle](/wiki/The_Jewish_Chronicle "The Jewish Chronicle")*, he is [spiritual but not religious](/wiki/Spiritual_but_not_religious "Spiritual but not religious"). He chose not to have a [Bar Mitzvah](/wiki/Bar_and_Bat_Mitzvah "Bar and Bat Mitzvah") as a child because of the custom of receiving [Bar Mitzvah gifts](/wiki/Bar_and_Bat_Mitzvah#Gifts "Bar and Bat Mitzvah"), explaining: "I felt as though a lot of kids were trying to cash in ... I just don't think it's the most respectful way to deal with God and beliefs and years and years and years of cultural heritage."[[9]](#cite_note-JC-9) Levine is a nephew of journalist and author [Timothy Noah](/wiki/Timothy_Noah "Timothy Noah"), and television producer and writer [Peter Noah](/wiki/Peter_Noah "Peter Noah").[[13]](#cite_note-noah-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Levine describes his family as "very musical"[[4]](#cite_note-stuart-4) and credits his mother with "start[ing] me out on the path."[[15]](#cite_note-cnn-15) He also attributes his mother's idols – [Simon & Garfunkel](/wiki/Simon_%26_Garfunkel "Simon & Garfunkel"), [Fleetwood Mac](/wiki/Fleetwood_Mac "Fleetwood Mac"), and [The Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") – to shaping his musical style, calling them "a huge part of my upbringing".[[9]](#cite_note-JC-9) Levine attended [Brentwood School](/wiki/Brentwood_School_(Los_Angeles) "Brentwood School (Los Angeles)"), where he met [Jesse Carmichael](/wiki/Jesse_Carmichael "Jesse Carmichael") and [Mickey Madden](/wiki/Mickey_Madden "Mickey Madden"), his future bandmates.[[16]](#cite_note-Moss-16) He carried his musical interests to high school, where he states he was "a little rebellious. I didn't want to do the things they were teaching me ... [music] consumed my every thought."[[4]](#cite_note-stuart-4)

## Bands

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Bands")]

### Kara's Flowers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Kara's Flowers")]

In February 1994, Levine, along with friends [Jesse Carmichael](/wiki/Jesse_Carmichael "Jesse Carmichael"), [Mickey Madden](/wiki/Mickey_Madden "Mickey Madden"), and Ryan Dusick formed garage band Kara's Flowers.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18) In 1995, the group played their first gig at the [Whisky a Go Go](/wiki/Whisky_a_Go_Go "Whisky a Go Go"), a nightclub in [West Hollywood, California](/wiki/West_Hollywood,_California "West Hollywood, California"), with Levine performing vocals and guitar.[[19]](#cite_note-kimpel-19) The band was discovered while they were performing in [Malibu](/wiki/Malibu,_California "Malibu, California") by independent producer Tommy Allen, who along with his partner [John DeNicola](/wiki/John_DeNicola "John DeNicola"), had them record an 11-track album.[[19]](#cite_note-kimpel-19) Owing to a string of industry showcases in Los Angeles, they were signed on to [Reprise Records](/wiki/Reprise_Records "Reprise Records") through producer [Rob Cavallo](/wiki/Rob_Cavallo "Rob Cavallo").[[20]](#cite_note-thompson-20)

In August 1997, the band released their first album, titled *[The Fourth World](/wiki/The_Fourth_World_(album) "The Fourth World (album)")*[[21]](#cite_note-appleford-21) and also appeared on an episode of the drama series *[Beverly Hills, 90210](/wiki/Beverly_Hills,_90210 "Beverly Hills, 90210")*.[[5]](#cite_note-lacey-5) Despite high expectations,[[19]](#cite_note-kimpel-19) it had little success, selling about 5,000 copies.[[22]](#cite_note-Bouwman_intrview/2004/April13_2_38_8.html-22) Reprise decided to drop the band after Cavallo's exit from the label.[[19]](#cite_note-kimpel-19)[[23]](#cite_note-cbs-23) Disappointed with the results of their album, the band broke up.[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-rosen-25) Later, Levine would say of the experience: "Kara's Flowers was just floating up the wall beneath the sticks. Make a record quickly, put it out. No touring base, no nothing. Just try to make it happen right out of the gate and it just doesn't work".[[26]](#cite_note-26)

### Maroon 5

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Maroon 5")]

Main article: [Maroon 5](/wiki/Maroon_5 "Maroon 5")

After the break up of Kara's Flowers, Levine, along with Carmichael, left Los Angeles to pursue higher studies at [Five Towns College](/wiki/Five_Towns_College "Five Towns College") in New York.[[19]](#cite_note-kimpel-19)[[27]](#cite_note-27) On MTV News, in 2002, he said: "That's when I started waking up to the whole hip-hop, R&B thing. We had friends named Chaos and Shit. It was *not* Brentwood High".[[28]](#cite_note-studiocitysun-28) They dropped out after a semester,[[29]](#cite_note-M5Bio-29) and reunited with Madden and Dusick to form a band once more.[[29]](#cite_note-M5Bio-29) They experimented with several styles, including [country](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") and [folk](/wiki/Folk_music "Folk music"), before deciding groove-based music would become their genre.[[16]](#cite_note-Moss-16) Levine explained the need for a makeover for the band: "We were just so sick of being a typical rock 'n' roll band ... I felt like I needed to look elsewhere for vocal inspiration."[[19]](#cite_note-kimpel-19) The band put together a demo that was rejected by several labels, before it caught the attention of [Octone Records](/wiki/Octone_Records "Octone Records") executives [James Diener](/wiki/James_Diener "James Diener"), Ben Berkman, and David Boxenbaum.[[25]](#cite_note-rosen-25) Following Berkman's advice, the band added a fifth member, [James Valentine](/wiki/James_Valentine_(musician) "James Valentine (musician)"), and was renamed Maroon 5. In an interview with [HitQuarters](/wiki/HitQuarters "HitQuarters"), Berkman explained that Levine "seemed to be a very shy, shoe-gazing type ... a fifth member could play the guitar to free up the singer [Levine], so he could be the star I perceived him to be".[[22]](#cite_note-Bouwman_intrview/2004/April13_2_38_8.html-22)

Around this time, Levine had been working as a writer's assistant on the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") television show *[Judging Amy](/wiki/Judging_Amy "Judging Amy")*; the program's producer [Barbara Hall](/wiki/Barbara_Hall_(TV_producer) "Barbara Hall (TV producer)") was his family friend.[[5]](#cite_note-lacey-5) While on the show, he would spend time writing songs about his ex-girlfriend. These songs were put into Maroon 5's debut album *[Songs About Jane](/wiki/Songs_About_Jane "Songs About Jane"),* which was released in June 2002. The album slowly gained airplay, and eventually became a [sleeper hit](/wiki/Sleeper_hit "Sleeper hit"), selling an estimated 10 million copies[[30]](#cite_note-leeds-30) and becoming the tenth best-selling album of 2004, two years after its release.[[31]](#cite_note-Rahman-31) In 2005, Maroon 5 won their first [Grammy Award](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award"), for [Best New Artist](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_New_Artist "Grammy Award for Best New Artist").[[32]](#cite_note-mcdermott-32) The next year, they won the [Grammy Award for Best Pop Performance by a Duo or Group with Vocals](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Performance_by_a_Duo_or_Group_with_Vocals "Grammy Award for Best Pop Performance by a Duo or Group with Vocals") for the second *Songs About Jane* single "[This Love](/wiki/This_Love_(Maroon_5_song) "This Love (Maroon 5 song)")".[[33]](#cite_note-bbc-33)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7e/Maroon_5_-_This_Love_live.jpg/250px-Maroon_5_-_This_Love_live.jpg)](/wiki/File:Maroon_5_-_This_Love_live.jpg)

Levine performing with Maroon 5 in 2007

By 2006, the band began recording again, and in May 2007, Maroon 5's second album *[It Won't Be Soon Before Long](/wiki/It_Won%27t_Be_Soon_Before_Long "It Won't Be Soon Before Long")* was released. Levine described the album as "a vast improvement", explaining: "I think this record is a little more self-confident and powerful lyrically".[[34]](#cite_note-Moran-34) To support the album, the band performed on a ten-date club tour which visited small venues in Europe and the United States from April to June 2007.[[35]](#cite_note-35)[[36]](#cite_note-36) The album and its lead, third and penultimate singles ("[Makes Me Wonder](/wiki/Makes_Me_Wonder "Makes Me Wonder")", "[Won't Go Home Without You](/wiki/Won%27t_Go_Home_Without_You "Won't Go Home Without You")" and "[If I Never See Your Face Again](/wiki/If_I_Never_See_Your_Face_Again "If I Never See Your Face Again")", respectively) each received Grammy nominations, although only "Makes Me Wonder" secured a win.[[37]](#cite_note-37)[[38]](#cite_note-grammys-38)

After winding down from a [world tour](/wiki/It_Won%27t_Be_Soon_Before_Long_Tour "It Won't Be Soon Before Long Tour") in support of *It Won't Be Soon Before Long*, the band began recording in Switzerland in 2009, in collaboration with record producer and songwriter [Robert John "Mutt" Lange](/wiki/Robert_John_%22Mutt%22_Lange "Robert John \"Mutt\" Lange"). Levine said Lange "worked me harder than anyone ever has".[[39]](#cite_note-rollingstone-39) In 2010, Maroon 5 released their third studio album, *[Hands All Over](/wiki/Hands_All_Over_(album) "Hands All Over (album)")*. The album did not initially meet expectations. In an interview with *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")*, Levine explained that the album suffered from being "all these disparate ideas and songs that didn't make any sense together".[[40]](#cite_note-wood-40) After the moderate success of the album's first three singles, the band released "[Moves like Jagger](/wiki/Moves_like_Jagger "Moves like Jagger")" (featuring [Christina Aguilera](/wiki/Christina_Aguilera "Christina Aguilera")) which Levine classified as "one of those songs that was definitely a risk; it's a bold statement".[[41]](#cite_note-benjamin-41) The single became a worldwide success; it was the ninth-best-selling digital single of 2011 with sales of 8.5 million copies and, as of 2012[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit), the [eighth-best-selling digital single of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_digital_singles "List of best-selling digital singles"). Levine later credited the song with "totally reviving the band".[[40]](#cite_note-wood-40)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/56/Adam_Levine2.JPG/250px-Adam_Levine2.JPG)](/wiki/File:Adam_Levine2.JPG)

Levine performing at the opening night of the Honda Civic Tour 2013

Since "Moves Like Jagger" was the first time Maroon 5 had collaborated with an outside writer, the band decided to attempt it again on their next album, entitled *[Overexposed](/wiki/Overexposed_(album) "Overexposed (album)")*.[[42]](#cite_note-rolling-42) Its title is supposedly an allusion to Levine's public ubiquity. In an interview with *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")*, he opined that is their most dance-driven album ever, commenting: "It's very much an old-fashioned disco tune. I have a love/hate relationship with it – but mostly I love it".[[43]](#cite_note-diehl-43) The album and its lead single "[Payphone](/wiki/Payphone_(song) "Payphone (song)")" gave Maroon 5 their second [Grammy Award for Best Pop Vocal Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Vocal_Album "Grammy Award for Best Pop Vocal Album") and [Best Pop Duo/Group Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Duo/Group_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Pop Duo/Group Performance") nominations.[[38]](#cite_note-grammys-38) In support of *Overexposed*, the band conducted the [Overexposed Tour](/wiki/Overexposed_Tour "Overexposed Tour") from 2012 to 2013 (with the European leg extending to 2014 due to scheduling conflicts),[[44]](#cite_note-copsey-44) and also headlined the [2013 Honda Civic Tour](/wiki/2013_Honda_Civic_Tour "2013 Honda Civic Tour"), which included *The Voice* contestant Tony Lucca.[[45]](#cite_note-jason-45)

In 2014, Maroon 5 continued their collaboration with [Ryan Tedder](/wiki/Ryan_Tedder "Ryan Tedder"), [Max Martin](/wiki/Max_Martin "Max Martin") and others to release their fifth studio album *[V](/wiki/V_(Maroon_5_album) "V (Maroon 5 album)")* (pronounced: "five"). Levine acknowledged that they followed the same song-writing process that they tried with *Overexposed*, saying: "We developed a really nice system on the last record — we found songs we were passionate about, developed them and put our stamp on them ... this time we kept it going but looked for different types of songs."[[46]](#cite_note-mwood-46) Five singles were released from it. In support of the album, the band undertook the [Maroon V Tour](/wiki/Maroon_V_Tour "Maroon V Tour"), which kicked off with a show in [Dallas](/wiki/Dallas "Dallas") in February 2015.[[47]](#cite_note-strecker-47)

In 2007, Levine had stated that he believed Maroon 5 was reaching its peak and might make one more album before disbanding.[[48]](#cite_note-green-48) He was quoted explaining: "Eventually I want to focus on being a completely different person because I don't know if I want to do this into my 40s and 50s and beyond". But in 2010, he dispelled any rumors of the band breaking up, saying:"I love what I do and think that, yes, it might be tiring and complicated at times [but] we don't have any plans on disbanding any time soon".[[49]](#cite_note-Concepcion-49) He has also turned down the idea of having a solo career, stating that "there will never be a solo record. I would sooner have another band".[[50]](#cite_note-freydkin-50) On February 10, 2017, Levine received a star on the [Hollywood Walk of Fame](/wiki/Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame "Hollywood Walk of Fame") for his contributions to the recording industry.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

## Other activities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other activities")]

### Musical collaborations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Musical collaborations")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/MuchMusic_Video_Awards_2007_661.jpg/250px-MuchMusic_Video_Awards_2007_661.jpg)](/wiki/File:MuchMusic_Video_Awards_2007_661.jpg)

Levine (right) with bandmate [Jesse Carmichael](/wiki/Jesse_Carmichael "Jesse Carmichael") in 2007

Levine has collaborated with several musical artists. In 2005, he was featured on the song "Live Again" by hip-hop duo [Ying Yang Twins](/wiki/Ying_Yang_Twins "Ying Yang Twins").[[52]](#cite_note-52) The same year, he appeared on [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West")'s album *[Late Registration](/wiki/Late_Registration "Late Registration")*, on the third single "[Heard 'Em Say](/wiki/Heard_%27Em_Say "Heard 'Em Say")",[[53]](#cite_note-corey-53) a collaboration Levine called "very pure and very easy". The song was created during an airplane flight that he and West shared,[[54]](#cite_note-coreymoss-54) and its refrain was later used for the Maroon 5 song "Nothing Lasts Forever" from *It Won't Be Soon Before Long*. He also appeared on [Alicia Keys](/wiki/Alicia_Keys "Alicia Keys")' third album *[Alicia Keys: MTV Unplugged](/wiki/Unplugged_(Alicia_Keys_album) "Unplugged (Alicia Keys album)")*, as part of the cover of The Rolling Stones song "[Wild Horses](/wiki/Wild_Horses_(The_Rolling_Stones_song) "Wild Horses (The Rolling Stones song)")".[[55]](#cite_note-55) Around the same time, he featured on fellow Octone Records singer [K'naan](/wiki/K%27naan "K'naan")'s single "Bang Bang".

In 2009, he recorded "Gotten", a song for [Slash](/wiki/Slash_(musician) "Slash (musician)")'s first solo album *[Slash](/wiki/Slash_(album) "Slash (album)")* (2010).[[56]](#cite_note-vinnicombe-56) In February 2010, he was among approximately 80 musicians who sang on the charity-single remake of "[We Are the World](/wiki/We_Are_the_World "We Are the World")", called "[We Are the World 25 for Haiti](/wiki/We_Are_the_World_25_for_Haiti "We Are the World 25 for Haiti")".[[57]](#cite_note-57) In 2011, he appeared on the [Gym Class Heroes](/wiki/Gym_Class_Heroes "Gym Class Heroes") song, "[Stereo Hearts](/wiki/Stereo_Hearts "Stereo Hearts")". Levine also worked with hip-hop artist [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent") on his song "[My Life](/wiki/My_Life_(50_Cent_song) "My Life (50 Cent song)")", recording the vocals almost two years before it was released as a single in 2012, which included rapper [Eminem](/wiki/Eminem "Eminem").[[58]](#cite_note-drake-58) In 2013, Levine wrote a song called "My Most Terrible Secret" performed by the cast of *[Community](/wiki/Community_(TV_series) "Community (TV series)")*, in the episode "[Intro to Felt Surrogacy](/wiki/Intro_to_Felt_Surrogacy "Intro to Felt Surrogacy")".[[59]](#cite_note-59)

In 2015, Levine was featured on the song "Painkiller" by [Rozzi Crane](/wiki/Rozzi_Crane "Rozzi Crane") and the duo, [R. City](/wiki/R._City "R. City")'s single "[Locked Away](/wiki/Locked_Away "Locked Away")".[[60]](#cite_note-y680-60) In 2016, Levine collaborated with [The Lonely Island](/wiki/The_Lonely_Island "The Lonely Island") for the song "I'm So Humble", on the soundtrack album *[Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping](/wiki/Popstar:_Never_Stop_Never_Stopping#Soundtrack "Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping")*, which they also appeared in the [film of the same name](/wiki/Popstar:_Never_Stop_Never_Stopping "Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping").[[61]](#cite_note-61) In April 2019, Levine and 29 other musical acts were featured on the [charity single](/wiki/Charity_single "Charity single") "[Earth](/wiki/Earth_(Lil_Dicky_song) "Earth (Lil Dicky song)")", which raises [climate change](/wiki/Climate_change "Climate change") awareness.[[62]](#cite_note-62) In late 2019, Levine collaborated with American actor and musician [Joe Pesci](/wiki/Joe_Pesci "Joe Pesci"), on his third album *Pesci... Still Singing*, with two songs "Baby Girl" and a cover of [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder")'s "[My Cherie Amour](/wiki/My_Cherie_Amour_(song) "My Cherie Amour (song)")".[[63]](#cite_note-63)

In 2020, Levine was featured on two songs "Trust Nobody", with rapper [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne") on his album *[Funeral](/wiki/Funeral_(Lil_Wayne_album) "Funeral (Lil Wayne album)")* and "Same Guy" by [Jack Harlow](/wiki/Jack_Harlow "Jack Harlow") on the album *[Thats What They All Say](/wiki/Thats_What_They_All_Say "Thats What They All Say")*.[[64]](#cite_note-64) Levine is also featured as a singer for his band's song "[She Will Be Loved](/wiki/She_Will_Be_Loved "She Will Be Loved")" in the music rhythm game, *[Band Hero](/wiki/Band_Hero "Band Hero")*.[[65]](#cite_note-pastorek-65) Levine has contributed with two songs for the soundtracks of the [John Carney](/wiki/John_Carney_(director) "John Carney (director)") films: "[Lost Stars](/wiki/Lost_Stars "Lost Stars")" in *[Begin Again](/wiki/Begin_Again_(film) "Begin Again (film)")* and "Go Now" in *[Sing Street](/wiki/Sing_Street "Sing Street")*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Television, film and media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Television, film and media")]

Levine has made four notable comic appearances on television. During 2007, he appeared in the 33rd-season premiere of *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")* in an [SNL Digital Short](/wiki/SNL_Digital_Short "SNL Digital Short") called *[Iran So Far](/wiki/Iran_So_Far "Iran So Far")*, performing with [Andy Samberg](/wiki/Andy_Samberg "Andy Samberg"), [Fred Armisen](/wiki/Fred_Armisen "Fred Armisen") and [Jake Gyllenhaal](/wiki/Jake_Gyllenhaal "Jake Gyllenhaal"). Levine played himself while singing a humorous [bridge](/wiki/Bridge_(music) "Bridge (music)") to a "love song" for [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/wiki/Mahmoud_Ahmadinejad "Mahmoud Ahmadinejad").[[66]](#cite_note-people-66) In 2008, he performed on [Comedy Central](/wiki/Comedy_Central "Comedy Central")'s "Night of Too Many Stars". He also had a cameo on *Jimmy Kimmel Live!* for the night of stars and endorsed [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama") in the [2008 Presidential Election](/wiki/2008_United_States_presidential_election "2008 United States presidential election"). In 2013, he hosted *Saturday Night Live* and featured alongside [Kendrick Lamar](/wiki/Kendrick_Lamar "Kendrick Lamar") on [Lonely Island's](/wiki/The_Lonely_Island "The Lonely Island") digital short "[YOLO](/wiki/YOLO_(The_Lonely_Island_song) "YOLO (The Lonely Island song)")", which parodies the [acronym](/wiki/Acronym "Acronym") for "You Only Live Once."[[67]](#cite_note-billboard-67) His hosting was generally disliked by reviewers, who called it "mediocre"[[68]](#cite_note-slims-68) and "subpar."[[69]](#cite_note-ryan-69) He appeared on [season 40](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live_(season_40)#Episodes "Saturday Night Live (season 40)") of *SNL* where he played [Freddie Mercury](/wiki/Freddie_Mercury "Freddie Mercury") and sang a snippet of "[Bohemian Rhapsody](/wiki/Bohemian_Rhapsody "Bohemian Rhapsody")" in the "Joan Rivers" sketch.[[70]](#cite_note-70)

From 2011 to 2019, Levine served as a contestant judge/coach on the reality talent television show, *[The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_TV_series) "The Voice (American TV series)")*.[[71]](#cite_note-ng-71) The contestants of his team who won in the series are [Javier Colon](/wiki/Javier_Colon "Javier Colon") ([season 1](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_season_1) "The Voice (American season 1)")), [Tessanne Chin](/wiki/Tessanne_Chin "Tessanne Chin") ([season 5](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_season_5) "The Voice (American season 5)"))[[72]](#cite_note-72) and [Jordan Smith](/wiki/Jordan_Smith_(musician) "Jordan Smith (musician)") ([season 9](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_season_9) "The Voice (American season 9)")). *The Voice* has been credited with reviving Maroon 5's "faltering" career after the sub-par sales of *Hands All Over* as well as increasing Levine's popularity. According to polling firm E-Poll Market Research, awareness of Levine has nearly tripled since he joined the show. He has also been described as the "breakout" star of the series, with #TeamAdam and @AdamLevine scoring a respective 203,000 and 2.14 million Twitter mentions in the show's third season, higher than all the other coaches. In 2013, *The Hollywood Reporter* estimated that Levine was paid $10–12 million for each season of *The Voice*.[[5]](#cite_note-lacey-5) In May 2019, Levine left the series after sixteen seasons and eight years.[[73]](#cite_note-73) In June 2024, it was revealed that Levine would return to *The Voice* for the [27th season](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_TV_series)_season_27 "The Voice (American TV series) season 27") which aired in the spring of 2025.[[74]](#cite_note-74) Levine will return for the 29th season, dubbed the "Battle of Champions", set to air in the spring of 2026.

In 2012, Levine appeared as a recurring character in *[American Horror Story: Asylum](/wiki/American_Horror_Story:_Asylum "American Horror Story: Asylum")*, the second season of the television series *[American Horror Story](/wiki/American_Horror_Story "American Horror Story")*.[[75]](#cite_note-75) He plays Leo Morrison, a newly-wed photographer visiting modern-day Briarcliff Manor, an insane asylum, on honeymoon with his wife, played by [Jenna Dewan](/wiki/Jenna_Dewan "Jenna Dewan"). The scenes were shot around his band's summer touring schedule. In an interview with *[E!](/wiki/E! "E!")*, he said of his role: "It sounded like so much fun and that's why I wanted to do it ... this sounds, like, hysterical, funny, dark and cool and right up my alley".[[76]](#cite_note-brit-76)[[77]](#cite_note-77) However, he admitted to not being a fan of the show nor horror genre in general, stating he didn't watch the episodes because "it's just so weird and disturbing".[[78]](#cite_note-post-78)

In June 2012, Levine was cast in the musical romance-drama film *[Begin Again](/wiki/Begin_Again_(film) "Begin Again (film)")* (originally titled *Can a Song Save Your Life?*). The film was directed by [John Carney](/wiki/John_Carney_(director) "John Carney (director)") and [Keira Knightley](/wiki/Keira_Knightley "Keira Knightley") and [Mark Ruffalo](/wiki/Mark_Ruffalo "Mark Ruffalo") acted in the lead roles. In it, he plays Dave Kohl, Knightley's songwriting partner and former boyfriend of five years, who leaves her behind on finding success in the music industry.[[79]](#cite_note-fleming-79)[[80]](#cite_note-rooney-80)[[81]](#cite_note-simone-81) The film premiered at the [2013 Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/2013_Toronto_International_Film_Festival "2013 Toronto International Film Festival") to generally favorable reviews from critics.[[82]](#cite_note-meta-82)

In November 2013, Levine was named *[People](/wiki/People_(magazine) "People (magazine)")* magazine's Sexiest Man Alive, becoming the first singer and the second non-actor (after [John F. Kennedy Jr.](/wiki/John_F._Kennedy_Jr. "John F. Kennedy Jr.")) to claim the title.[[83]](#cite_note-coulton-83) He was ranked No. 41 on *[Glamour](/wiki/Glamour_(magazine) "Glamour (magazine)")'s* "Sexiest Men of 2012" list.[[84]](#cite_note-84) In 2008, he appeared on *People's* "Single and Sexy Men" list.[[85]](#cite_note-85) He was elected TV's Most Crushworthy Male Reality Host/Judge in a poll held by [Zap2it](/wiki/Zap2it "Zap2it").[[86]](#cite_note-86) In April 2012, *Shalom Life* ranked him Number 7 on its list of "Top 50 Hottest Jewish men in the world".[[87]](#cite_note-87) Levine stripped naked for [testicular cancer](/wiki/Testicular_cancer "Testicular cancer") awareness for a centerfold in *[Cosmopolitan](/wiki/Cosmopolitan_(magazine) "Cosmopolitan (magazine)")* UK's February 2011 issue.[[88]](#cite_note-Huffington_Post-88)

### 222 Productions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 222 Productions")]

In 2013, Levine started a production company 222 Productions and the first project was *[Sugar](/wiki/Sugar_(2018_TV_series) "Sugar (2018 TV series)")* (2018), a [YouTube Premium](/wiki/YouTube_Premium "YouTube Premium") web television series which was inspired by the music video for the [Maroon 5 song of the same name](/wiki/Sugar_(Maroon_5_song) "Sugar (Maroon 5 song)"). It follows music artists to crash events for unsuspecting fans.[[89]](#cite_note-89) The company produced a reality competition series *[Songland](/wiki/Songland "Songland")*, which premiered on [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") on May 28, 2019, where Levine served as executive producer. The company signed a deal with Wheelhouse Entertainment.[[90]](#cite_note-90)

### Business ventures and endorsements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Business ventures and endorsements")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a0/Adam_Levine_with_guitar_at_2007_MyCokeFest_in_Atlanta.JPG/250px-Adam_Levine_with_guitar_at_2007_MyCokeFest_in_Atlanta.JPG)](/wiki/File:Adam_Levine_with_guitar_at_2007_MyCokeFest_in_Atlanta.JPG)

Levine playing the First Act 222 Guitar, which he helped design

In October 2008, Levine collaborated with [First Act](/wiki/First_Act "First Act") to create the First Act 222 Guitar, which was fashioned to his specifications. The guitar was sold via [Target](/wiki/Target_(store) "Target (store)") stores.[[91]](#cite_note-YT-91) Two years later he launched his own fashion line, entitled "222", at the Project Trade Show in [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas_Valley "Las Vegas Valley"). The collection features jeans, basic T-shirts and leather jackets. The venture was organized in partnership with his father, Fred Levine (who operates a chain of specialty boutiques), and his cousin, Sami Cooper.[[92]](#cite_note-92)[[93]](#cite_note-hagwood-93)

In June 2011, Levine took part in an educational campaign to raise awareness of [attention deficit hyperactivity disorder](/wiki/Attention_deficit_hyperactivity_disorder "Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder") (ADHD). The project, titled "Own It", was created by [Shire](/wiki/Shire_plc "Shire plc") and organized in collaboration with the Attention Deficit Disorder Association (ADDA), Children and Adults with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (CHADHD). The project targets people who were previously diagnosed with the disorder, focusing on how it may continue into adulthood. Levine, who himself was diagnosed with ADHD as a teenager, said: "This campaign is important to me because it can help young adults and adults realize that there's a chance they may still have ADHD if they had it as a kid".[[94]](#cite_note-94)[[95]](#cite_note-hamilton-95) In connection to this, he wrote an article in *[ADDitude Magazine](/wiki/ADDitude_Magazine "ADDitude Magazine")* about his personal experience with it.[[96]](#cite_note-levine-96)

Levine founded his own record label, [222 Records](/wiki/222_Records "222 Records") in February 2012. He stated that he was inspired to start the label to sign on [Rozzi Crane](/wiki/Rozzi_Crane "Rozzi Crane"), an [USC](/wiki/University_of_Southern_California "University of Southern California") music student he discovered through a mutual friend.[[97]](#cite_note-simpson-97) She became the first singer signed on to the label, followed by *[Glee](/wiki/Glee_(TV_series) "Glee (TV series)")* actor [Matthew Morrison](/wiki/Matthew_Morrison "Matthew Morrison"), Mexican artist [Diego Boneta](/wiki/Diego_Boneta "Diego Boneta"), and *The Voice* [season 2](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_season_2) "The Voice (American season 2)") contestant and part of Team Adam, the singer [Tony Lucca](/wiki/Tony_Lucca "Tony Lucca"). It was reported that he was negotiating further with potential distributors, as well as organizing staff, to operate as a full-fledged record company with departments such as marketing, radio and publicity.[[98]](#cite_note-98)[[99]](#cite_note-99)

In September 2012, Levine was in the [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines") to collaborate with the clothing company [Bench](/wiki/Bench_(Philippine_clothing_brand) "Bench (Philippine clothing brand)"); they launched the menswear collection.[[100]](#cite_note-100) In January 2013, Levine announced he would be enter a partnership with [Sears Holdings](/wiki/Sears_Holdings "Sears Holdings") to launch a multi-department lifestyle brand of apparel and accessories collections.[[101]](#cite_note-lazare-101) The company owns [Kmart](/wiki/Kmart_(United_States) "Kmart (United States)") and ShopYourWay, a shopping social platform; it also includes rapper [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj") in the same contract. The menswear collection was launched on October 1 that year and conducts business via 500 Kmart stores across the US, as well as online.[[102]](#cite_note-102) In an official statement, Levine said: "Partnering with ShopYourWay to develop this line was an exciting opportunity for me and I am really looking forward to diving into the process of designing an apparel and accessory collection".[[103]](#cite_note-Pous-103) In an interview with *People*, he commented further, "it was cool that they really promoted creative control. I like to be involved with process rather than just phoning it in".[[104]](#cite_note-104) Later, Levine became a celebrity spokesperson for [Proactiv](/wiki/Proactiv "Proactiv"). In the commercial, Levine shares details about his acne experiences in high school, and promotes Proactiv Plus.[[105]](#cite_note-Rose-105)

Levine collaborated with ID Perfumes to create his debut [eponymous](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/eponym "wikt:eponym") scent.[[106]](#cite_note-Wischhover-106) The line was launched at the Premiere Fragrance Installation in Los Angeles in February 2013.[[107]](#cite_note-Schreffle-107) The fragrance range, consisting of scents for both for men and women, is sold through [Macy's](/wiki/Macy%27s "Macy's") department stores in microphone-shaped bottles. Speaking at its launch, Levine said: "The task was to make something that I would wear. So that was a process and we finally came to a great conclusion and it smells great"[[108]](#cite_note-nessif-108)[[109]](#cite_note-naughton-109) The fragrance garnered media attention for contradicting his tweet the previous year, in which he said that he wanted to "put an official ban on celebrity fragrances. Punishable by death from this point forward".[[110]](#cite_note-110) In January 2020, Levine announced that he is the new Ambassador of the brand [Shure](/wiki/Shure "Shure") for the wireless earphones and headphones, the Aonic 215 and 50, is available on electronic stores on April 2, 2020.[[111]](#cite_note-111)[[112]](#cite_note-112)[[113]](#cite_note-113)

In 2021, Levine and his wife Prinsloo founded a tequila company called Calirosa. The brand's tequila bottles were made available from December 2021.[[114]](#cite_note-114)[[115]](#cite_note-115)

## Artistry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Artistry")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/44/Adam_Levine_from_Maroon_5_cropped.JPG/250px-Adam_Levine_from_Maroon_5_cropped.JPG)](/wiki/File:Adam_Levine_from_Maroon_5_cropped.JPG)

Levine performs in Washington, D.C.

Levine's interest in music started at around ten years of age, when he first started playing the guitar. He found music as an outlet for his feelings, stating: "I picked up a guitar and that was it. I fell so madly in love with it, it's all I did".[[4]](#cite_note-stuart-4) He performed his first professional gig at [The Troubadour](/wiki/The_Troubadour,_West_Hollywood "The Troubadour, West Hollywood") when he was twelve, but he was so nervous that he played with his back to the audience.[[5]](#cite_note-lacey-5)[[116]](#cite_note-hefferman-116) Throughout his childhood, he had a wide range of musical influences, including [The Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles"), [Fleetwood Mac](/wiki/Fleetwood_Mac "Fleetwood Mac"), [The Who](/wiki/The_Who "The Who"), [Pearl Jam](/wiki/Pearl_Jam "Pearl Jam"), [Soundgarden](/wiki/Soundgarden "Soundgarden"), [Alice in Chains](/wiki/Alice_in_Chains "Alice in Chains"), and [Nirvana](/wiki/Nirvana_(band) "Nirvana (band)"), and, in high school, [Bob Marley](/wiki/Bob_Marley "Bob Marley"), [Bill Withers](/wiki/Bill_Withers "Bill Withers"), [Al Green](/wiki/Al_Green "Al Green"), [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder"), [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye"),[[117]](#cite_note-robinson-117) [Phish](/wiki/Phish "Phish")[[118]](#cite_note-118) and [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson").[[119]](#cite_note-RS-119) He has also incorporated elements of [The Police](/wiki/The_Police_(band) "The Police (band)") and [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)") into his music.[[120]](#cite_note-michelson-120) In an interview with *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")*, he explained the diversity of his influences: "I love every single kind of music ... even the most saccharine, sugary pop song can be the greatest thing ever. But so can a 25-minute crazy avant-garde fusion gnarly [Herbie Hancock](/wiki/Herbie_Hancock "Herbie Hancock") jam from the '70s".[[121]](#cite_note-billboard2-121)

Levine remembers that listening to "[Are You That Somebody?](/wiki/Are_You_That_Somebody%3F "Are You That Somebody?")" by [Aaliyah](/wiki/Aaliyah "Aaliyah") convinced him to pursue a more soulful sound than that of the band he was performing with at the time, Kara's Flowers.[[21]](#cite_note-appleford-21) His move to New York introduced him to a new music scene that involved [hip-hop](/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop"), [R&B](/wiki/R%26B "R&B"), [gospel](/wiki/Gospel_music "Gospel music") and [soul music](/wiki/Soul_music "Soul music").[[28]](#cite_note-studiocitysun-28) He took to changing his musical style, extensively emulating Stevie Wonder.[[120]](#cite_note-michelson-120) Subsequently, *Songs About Jane* was released, deemed "bluesy funk"[[122]](#cite_note-wilson-122) and similar to the sound of English [pop rock](/wiki/Pop_rock "Pop rock") band [Busted](/wiki/Busted_(band) "Busted (band)").[[123]](#cite_note-sullivan-123) Critics also drew comparisons between Levine and [Jamiroquai](/wiki/Jamiroquai "Jamiroquai") singer [Jay Kay](/wiki/Jay_Kay "Jay Kay").[[124]](#cite_note-hoard-124)

While earlier work was deemed "vaguely funky white-soul"[[124]](#cite_note-hoard-124) and "rock", recent ones have been judged to have a more reggae, anthemic pop sound,[[125]](#cite_note-Young-125) evoking comparisons to [Coldplay](/wiki/Coldplay "Coldplay").[[126]](#cite_note-adams-126) Levine refuses to fit his music into a genre, saying: "There's so much variety in music, it's silly to belong to a specific club and try to sound a certain way".[[120]](#cite_note-michelson-120) He considers himself an orthodox lyricist sticking to conventional themes, acknowledging: "Romance, love, the lack thereof are still very big themes. I haven't figured out a way to use everything yet. As a songwriter, I'm still limited to that one thing."[[21]](#cite_note-appleford-21) He also claims he does not like mincing words, stating in a *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* interview: "I was so sick of typical lyrics like 'Ooh, baby' and 'I love you' and all this vague shit. I thought the more explicit I got without being totally explicit was a nice approach".[[28]](#cite_note-studiocitysun-28)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d7/Adam_Levine_performing_in_Sydney.jpg/250px-Adam_Levine_performing_in_Sydney.jpg)](/wiki/File:Adam_Levine_performing_in_Sydney.jpg)

Levine performing in Sydney, Australia in February 2019

Levine is a [countertenor](/wiki/Countertenor "Countertenor") and [tenor](/wiki/Tenor "Tenor"), with a 4 octave vocal range[[127]](#cite_note-127)[[128]](#cite_note-pareles-128) and has been noted for his [falsettos](/wiki/Falsetto "Falsetto").[[129]](#cite_note-fetters-129) [Salon](/wiki/Salon_(website) "Salon (website)") wrote: "When he's crooning come-ons, his voice lends the music a satisfying lewdness, a sense of sticky physicality that gives his snaky hooks a pheromonal urgency."[[130]](#cite_note-130) In a review of *It Won't Be Soon Before Long*, *[Entertainment Weekly](/wiki/Entertainment_Weekly "Entertainment Weekly")* described his vocals as "smug, R&B-slick deadpan ... there's a twisted logic to his dispassionate delivery".[[131]](#cite_note-drumming-131) In another review, [Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic") wrote "he knows that he's a pop guy, somewhat in the tradition of [Hall & Oates](/wiki/Hall_%26_Oates "Hall & Oates"), but he isn't trying to be retro, he's ... making records that are melodic, stylish, and soulful".[[132]](#cite_note-erlewine-132) In a review of the 2013 Honda Civic Tour, *[The Boston Globe](/wiki/The_Boston_Globe "The Boston Globe")* also commented positively on his on-stage presence, which "exude[s] a sense of up-for-anything playfulness ... combined with a rock solid work ethic and a clear love for their audiences and performing".[[133]](#cite_note-rodman-133)

Levine's popularity outside of his musical work has seen him tagged as a "stand-alone star,"[[134]](#cite_note-markovitz-134) which critics say have pushed other members of Maroon 5 to the backseat, even in their music.[[135]](#cite_note-stewart-135) Their guitarist Valentine noted that his vocals were a central aspect around which their music revolved.[[136]](#cite_note-lopez-136) Conversely, others opine that Levine's fame has been a boost to the band, with *[Paper](/wiki/Paper_(magazine) "Paper (magazine)")* writing: "Maroon 5 has managed to ebb and flow with the times ... thanks in no small part to their frontman's uncanny ability to be extremely entertaining".[[137]](#cite_note-spaner-137) *[Delta Sky](/wiki/Delta_Sky_Magazine "Delta Sky Magazine")* described him as "a natural, if slightly neurotic, leading man".[[138]](#cite_note-clayton-138) He claims that the image was consciously cultivated, explaining: "We talked about it a long time ago and decided I would step out, for us, not for me or my own ego ... We wanted there to be a frontman."[[50]](#cite_note-freydkin-50)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Personal life")]

### Relationships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Relationships")]

In early 2010, while performing at the *[Sports Illustrated](/wiki/Sports_Illustrated "Sports Illustrated")* Swimsuit Issue release party in Las Vegas, Levine met Russian *Sports Illustrated* Swimsuit Issue model [Anne Vyalitsyna](/wiki/Anne_Vyalitsyna "Anne Vyalitsyna") and they soon began a relationship.[[139]](#cite_note-everett-139) They ended the relationship in April 2012 in an "amicable and supportive manner".[[140]](#cite_note-140)

In May 2012, Levine began dating [Behati Prinsloo](/wiki/Behati_Prinsloo "Behati Prinsloo"), a Namibian [Victoria's Secret](/wiki/Victoria%27s_Secret "Victoria's Secret") model.[[141]](#cite_note-ravitz-141)[[142]](#cite_note-142) The couple married on July 19, 2014, with [Jonah Hill](/wiki/Jonah_Hill "Jonah Hill") officiating the wedding.[[143]](#cite_note-143)[[144]](#cite_note-144) Levine and Prinsloo have two daughters, Dusty Rose (b. September 21, 2016)[[145]](#cite_note-145) and Gio Grace (b. February 15, 2018).[[146]](#cite_note-146)[[147]](#cite_note-147) On January 28, 2023, Levine and Prinsloo had a third child, who is their first son.[[148]](#cite_note-148)[[149]](#cite_note-149)

Levine and Hill have been friends since early childhood, having met in grade school.[[150]](#cite_note-150) Hill's brother, Jordan Feldstein, managed Maroon 5 until Feldstein's death.[[151]](#cite_note-151) Despite attending different high schools, Levine has been friends with American actress [Kate Hudson](/wiki/Kate_Hudson "Kate Hudson") since they were teenagers.[[152]](#cite_note-152)

### Activism and philanthropy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Activism and philanthropy")]

Levine is supportive of gay rights. In 2011, Levine made a video[[153]](#cite_note-153) on Maroon 5's official YouTube account in support of the [It Gets Better Project](/wiki/It_Gets_Better_Project "It Gets Better Project"). In January 2012, he announced that Maroon 5 had changed the location of their post-Grammy Awards show because of the "unnamed Los Angeles restaurant's backing of [Proposition 8](/wiki/California_Proposition_8 "California Proposition 8")".[[154]](#cite_note-huffington2012-154) He has a gay brother.[[154]](#cite_note-huffington2012-154)

In July 2020, Levine and Prinsloo collaborated with [Ferrari](/wiki/Ferrari "Ferrari") and [Save the Children](/wiki/Save_the_Children "Save the Children") to raise funds to support U.S. education programs during the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[155]](#cite_note-155)

### Allegations of misconduct

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adam_Levine&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Allegations of misconduct")]

In 2013, Levine was mentioned in a [hostile work environment](/wiki/Hostile_work_environment "Hostile work environment") lawsuit filed in [Los Angeles Superior Court](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Superior_Court "Los Angeles Superior Court") by an unnamed security guard who claimed that [Universal Music Publishing Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Publishing_Group "Universal Music Publishing Group")'s Santa Monica location was "infiltrated with pervasive drug use where you could smell marijuana seeping from various offices and openly used in common areas, and lounges". The guard claimed that when she complained about the cannabis smoke coming from one of the studios, she was told that "it's Adam Levine—if he wants to come to the lobby and do a line of cocaine on the floor, it's OK". In an official statement to *The Hollywood Reporter*, UMPG (Universal Music Publishing group) described the allegations as "absurd".[[156]](#cite_note-gardner-156)[[157]](#cite_note-giles-157)

In September 2022, Levine was accused by at least four women of [cheating](/wiki/Infidelity "Infidelity") on Prinsloo with them. DMs, texts, and [TikTok](/wiki/TikTok "TikTok") videos were presented as evidence.[[158]](#cite_note-158)[[159]](#cite_note-159) Levine denied having an affair, but has admitted to "crossing" the line and has "addressed ... and taken proactive steps to remedy this with my family".[[160]](#cite_note-160)
